# ragornot

> Compare retrieval architectures, track real cost and latency, and follow the AI, LLM, and RAG conversation.

ragornot is a web app by Rohit Sarna that lets users compare four retrieval strategies:
- **Flat (Lexical)**: global BM25-style chunk ranking across all 116 indexed AWS documentation pages
- **Hierarchical**: document → section → chunk narrowing before ranking, improving precision
- **LLM-only**: direct answer from AWS Bedrock (Claude Haiku) with no retrieval grounding
- **RAG**: hierarchical retrieval followed by Bedrock generation, combining accuracy and grounding

The live benchmark runs all four modes against the same query and reports latency, confidence, quality (query-term coverage), and cost per query. All generative calls go to the real AWS Lambda backend.

## Pages

- /news — AI, LLM, RAG, cost, and sustainability news aggregated from arXiv, Hacker News, and publisher RSS feeds. Refreshed every 6 hours via GitHub Actions.
- /explore — Interactive search over 116 AWS documentation pages with mode selection and live results.
- /benchmark — Live multi-query benchmark runner comparing all four retrieval modes side by side, plus an impact analytics section showing cost and CO₂ estimates.

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/rohitsarna/ragornot
- Author: Rohit Sarna (https://github.com/rohitsarna)
